questions.content: Drawing and singing were among her many _______.
We need to ensure a smooth _______ between the old system and the new one.
Success in the task will _______ his reputation as an international statesman.
There are various _______ open to you.
You should be _______ of yourself for telling such lies.
We'll send you a copy as soon as it becomes _______.
The conference will address the broad issues _______ with e-commerce.
A(n) _______ computer system is an open door to criminals.
The _______ teaching methods include letting the children decide what to study.
There must be some form of external _______ of the data.
I _______ the scholarship program, but I haven't heard anything back yet.
I can pursue it now at my own pace since I am no longer _______ making it my career.
You go on ahead. I just need to _______ some work before I head home.
The description of the course really _______ what I wanted in a literature class.
Day to day I _______ and do what feels good.
Be sure that you're using a design that works with your _______.
I can't take money from a charity like that because it _______ all of my core principle.
Ever since the day when she had faced her own fears she had _______ her principle.

Studies have even shown that feelings of authenticity can go hand in hand with _______ psychological and social benefits: higher self-esteem, greater well-being, better romantic relationships and enhanced work performance.
Although most people would define authenticity as acting in accordance with your idiosyncratic set of _______ and qualities,

research has shown that people feel most authentic when they _______ to a particular set of socially approved qualities, such as being extrovert, emotionally stable, conscientious, intellectual and agreeable.
This is the paradox of authenticity: in order to reap the many benefits of feeling authentic, you may have to _______ your true nature.
From a psychological science standpoint, a person is considered authentic if he or she meets certain _______.
Authentic people have considerable self knowledge and are _______ to learn more about themselves.
They are equally interested in understanding their strengths and weaknesses, and they are willing to _______ reflect on feedback regardless of whether it is flattering or unflattering.
Most importantly, authentic people behave in line with their unique values and qualities even if those idiosyncrasies may _______ with social conventions or other external influences.

Afterwards, they complete _______ — daily or once a week over a period of multiple weeks — about the extent to which their behavior reflected their qualities and the extent to which they felt authentic.
We would expect that people feel most authentic on days where their behavior closely _______ their unique pattern of values and qualities.
自由是社会主义核心价值观中社会层面的第一个词，饱含了大家对美好社会的向往。自由是什么，想必大家都不陌生。其本义是由自己做主，依从自己的想法、意志、愿望行事，不受外来限制和约束。只是在我们现实生活中，不管不顾、随心所欲做自己想要的事情，这肯定是不可取的。我们所追求的自由，是一种每个人在规则也就是法律的约束下，能够不受外部因素干扰，安心追求自己的理想，尽情挥洒自己的才华。
questions.answer: Freedom is the first word in the social dimension of core socialist values; it embodies our aspirations for a better society. It is certain that we are all familiar with what freedom is. Its original meaning is to make one's own decisions, follow one's own thoughts, will and wishes, and act without external restrictions and limitations. It is just that in real life, it is definitely undesirable to do whatever we want regardless of the consequences. The freedom we seek is a kind of freedom that everyone can pursue their own ideals and give full play to their talents without interference from external factors and under the constraint of rules and laws.

questions.content: I sensed that his mind was already made up, but he needed validation about pursuing a path that is not generally thought of as a professional success.
questions.answer: 我感觉到他已经下定了决心，但他追求的是一条通常不被认为是职业成功的道路，对此，他需要得到认可。

questions.content: I put off making a decision by taking a research scientist position in the lab of a new PI so that I could see the job up close, with all its benefits and struggles.
questions.answer: 我先应聘到一位新晋课题负责人的实验室担任科研研究员，借此暂缓做职业抉择，近距离亲身感受这份工作，体会其中的种种利好与难处。

questions.content: When there is a mismatch between what society considers successful and our own definitions of success, we need to hold fast to our beliefs and follow our own road to personal satisfaction.
questions.answer: 当社会对成功的定义和我们自己对成功的定义不匹配时，我们需要坚守自己的信念，走自己的道路，让我们自己满意。

questions.content: What do you think of Elective Courses in College? Give your supporting ideas. You should write at least 120 words but no more than 180 words.
He _______ to be treated with respect.
They have faith that people can change or learn from _______ experiences.
Resisting _______ goodies becomes a measure of your success.
The partners were delighted with the _______ of their work.
The study has _______ the toxic effects of the pollutant.
He was _______ a full professorship and a significant pay raise.
He did it out of a feeling of _______ obligation.
They have a reputation for good service and _______.
The children were evaluated for such _______ as determination and self-management.
Pride can lead to _______ and a sense of superiority.
Our organization has been _______ peace in this region for the last ten years.
I may _______ once in a while with laundry and trips to the store for him.
We are _______ helping teachers provide their students with effective feedback.
We should not _______ about things we can't have absolute knowledge about.
Do you _______ those temptations or do you struggle to fight them?
Learn to _______ others, expect respect from others, and respect yourself.
I cannot _______ my friends just because my brother is a flake.
Each day I reflect on myself, whether I have failed to _______ to my friends.
Do you know anyone who has _______ to you he or she has over-reported the number of hours worked to pad a paycheck?
Some may think of these "white lies", or small instances of dishonest behavior, as relatively harmless, a slight _______ lapse when compared with full-scale corporate fraud.
Researchers have studied the potential financial and legal consequences of such small instances of _______ as padding expense reports and pilfering pens.
We examined the possibility that small instances of dishonest behavior may have unintended _______ for our emotional
_______ — that it seeps into our ability to read others' emotions.
In a series of studies, we concluded that an act of deceit can _______ a person's ability to interact with peers, even those removed from the original lie.
Specifically, we found that when people _______ in dishonest behavior, they are less likely to see themselves as having any kind of relationship with other people.
and are _______ less accurate in judging the emotions of others.
The investigation is a critical step in understanding the underlying _______ dynamics in organizations, because work relationships can be productive or corrosive.
The ability to _______ read and respond to others' emotional states generates supportive, prosocial and compassionate behavior.

中华民族在历史的长河中累积下的优良道德品质不可胜数，而诚信是其中最引人注目的一颗明珠。诚信即诚实守信，是从古至今几千年来传承下来的优良道德传统，涉及个人的操守、行业的自律，乃至社会的风尚和国家的气度，它强调的是君子立言，一诺千金；君子处事，诚信为本。以诚实守信为荣，这不仅仅是中华民族每个公民的立身处世之本，更是我们民族得以繁衍生息、欣欣向荣的强大精神支柱。将诚信二字内化于心，外化于行，切实践行社会主义核心价值观是每一个中国公民的责任。
questions.answer: The Chinese nation has accumulated innumerable fine moral qualities in the long course of its history, and integrity is the most striking among them. Integrity, namely honesty and trustworthiness, is a fine moral tradition inherited from ancient times to the present day, involving personal conduct, industry's self-regulation, and even social mores and a national demeanor. It emphasizes that a gentleman is true to his words and his conduct is based on integrity. Taking pride in integrity is not only the foundation for every citizen of the Chinese nation to get on in the world, but also the strong spiritual pillar for our nation to prosper and thrive. It is the responsibility of every Chinese citizen to internalize the word integrity and to practice the core socialist values.

questions.content: It is something that can be faked, but there are some ways to tell that someone has real integrity and are not just faking it.
questions.answer: 这是一种可以伪造的东西，但有一些方法可以判断一个人有真正的诚信，而不是装出来的。

questions.content: Additionally, everyone should also strive to surround themselves with people who have true integrity.
questions.answer: 此外，每个人还应该努力结交真正诚信的人。

questions.content: Someone with integrity knows that things are not always as they seem and that each person has a unique point of view that deserves to be heard.
questions.answer: 诚信之人懂得，事情并非像表面看起来那样，每个人都有独特的观点，值得被倾听。

questions.content: What do you think of the pressure or obstacle you faced? And how do you address it? You should write at least 120 words but no more than 180 words.
We should _______ the habit of thinking of what we are doing.
Movies have shaped our _______ of many historical events.
You should use the _______ skills acquired from previous work.
All the _______ in the cooking contest received a free cookbook.
The survey indicates that tourist numbers have _______ in recent years.
The company's business dealings need to be more _______.
There is a popular _______ that too much exercise is bad for you.
Landslides and bad weather are continuing to _______ the arrival of relief supplies to the area.
The recession has forced a lot of companies to cut down on graduate _______.
He was one of the most creative and _______ engineers of his generation.
He prefers to eat out at a restaurant that _______ seafood.
Women officers _______ 13 percent of the police force.
She felt she had been _______ because of her age.
He _______ a job with the local newspaper a month ago.
We missed the train, and _______ that we had to wait for two hours for the next one.
She has _______ for evening classes at the community college.
The agreement has _______ to cut greenhouse gas emissions worldwide.
The professor has been _______ stealing his student's ideas and publishing them.
Boys, researchers found, _______ park areas like basketball courts and playground equipment.
To make the park more appealing to girls, park designers _______ what amounted to gender-segregated spaces, installing volleyball and badminton courts for girls.
and dividing open areas into more _______ spaces with landscaping.
That _______ in numbers meant that in competition for park space, boys were now less likely to crowd out girls.
Having _______ strategies to plan and build cities for women since the early 1990s,
Vienna required each of its agencies to introduce strategic plans and _______ to support the explicit inclusion of the needs, concerns and lived experiences of women
to shape more equitable policy _______ for residents of all genders.
It created spaces where _______ between genders were less likely, without addressing the root of the problem: the unequal gender power dynamics between boys and girls.
This kind of design solution for gender equity in public space is not _______.
Because the typical user of urban space has, since the advent of modernism, been the young, _______ male, this baseline informs every other user group's experience of the city.
Worse still, a new survey shows that the number of women in the tech sector has barely moved over the past 10 years despite an industry-wide push.
questions.answer: 更糟糕的是，一项新的调查显示，尽管整个科技行业都在进步，但在过去10年里，科技行业的女性人数几乎没有变化。

questions.content: More than 8 out of 10 female millennials in Britain say that they seek out employers with a strong record on diversity, equality and inclusion, according to research.
questions.answer: 研究显示，英国超过80%的千禧一代的女性表示，她们会寻找在多样性、平等和包容性方面有良好记录的雇主。

questions.content: The issues around female leadership aren't likely to be fixed in the next 10 years, but the symptoms are being acknowledged, which is a good thing.
questions.answer: 女性领导的问题不可能在未来10年内得到解决，但人们正在意识到这些问题，这是一件好事。

questions.content: 平等是人和人之间的一种关系、人对人的一种态度，是人类的终极理想之一。人和人之间的平等，不是指物质上的“相等”或“平均”，而是在精神上互相理解、互相尊重，把对方当成和自己一样的人来待。现代社会的进步就是人和人之间从不平等走向平等的过程，是平等逐渐实现的过程。人类只有更好地理解“平等”才能更好地实践“平等”。
questions.answer: Equality is a relationship between human beings and a human-to-human attitude. It is one of mankind's ultimate ideals. Equality between human beings does not mean "equal" or "average" in material terms, but rather mutual understanding and respect in spiritual terms, treating each other as if we were the same. The progress of modern society is the process of moving from inequality to equality between people, which is a process of gradual realization of equality. Only through a better understanding of equality can we put equality into practice.

questions.content: Write an essay on Your Ideal Job. You should write at least 120 words but no more than 180 words.
Most countries have enthusiastically _______ the concept of high-speed railways.
Under the new _______, coach drivers must take a break every four hours.
The newspaper made the _______ move of publishing the names of the men involved.
The value of exports _______ last year, recording an all-time high.
He was determined to _______ the achievements of his older brothers.
The presentation was a(n) _______ effort by all the children in the class.
According to the report, the two buildings were _______ equal in size.
The system records all _______ between the company and its suppliers.
John showed a high level of _______ from an early age.
The law will _______ employers to provide health insurance.
What you will see is a constant effort to improve the way we _______ to customers.
The bus or train could _______ or slow down in accordance with traffic and passenger loads.
It's a mistake to think of Florida only _______ its tourist attractions.
Only about a third of that increase can be _______ weather problems.
It is reported that public spending continues to rise _______.
Companies that are successful in the _______ have long realized the importance of localization.
The company has been _______ a family business to a multi-million-pound operation.
The city's problems are due, at least _______, to its geographical location.
Major Hollywood movies have given a superficial impression of this technological _______.
But artificial intelligence is a much broader _______ than the popular notion of it.
These processes are later _______ with reference to the needs of technology.
Artificial intelligence has _______ its prominence by helping increase the size, speed and variety of big data collected and can also perform various functions related to it.
This is because managing statistical data on a large scale can be a(n) _______ time-consuming process for humans.
But with the help of artificial intelligence, complicated tasks like the identification of patterns in the data can be performed in a quicker and more _______ manner than by humans.
This helps businesses gain better insight into their data, which makes AI highly _______ to any other way of proceeding.
The study of artificial intelligence is ideal for _______ the very nature of human intelligence itself and how it connects to technology and engineering.
Artificial intelligence can be said to be at the _______ of a majority of the technological developments taking place currently.
It plays a(n) _______ role in almost all fields of life.
Another major shift has taken place within Chinese fintech companies, which increasingly use technology to deliver services that Western firms don't offer.
questions.answer: 中国金融科技公司内部发生了另一个重大转变，它们不断利用技术来提供西方公司不能提供的服务。

questions.content: He attributes the success of Chinese tech start-ups, in part, to Chinese companies' willingness to work closely with global partners.
questions.answer: 他将中国科技创业公司的成功，部分归功于中国公司愿意与全球合作伙伴紧密合作。

questions.content: China has transformed itself from being seen as "the world's factory" to becoming a hive of technology innovation, with ideas that leapfrog the West.
questions.answer: 从被视为“世界工厂”到“科技创新的蜂巢”，中国已经完成了自身的转变，其理念超越了西方。

questions.content: 技术创新是经济增长和人类进步的根本动力。它在科技时代和全球经济中占据了中心地位。技术进步有助于提高经济产出水平，并能提供改变人类生活和能力的新产品和服务。创新产品提高了人们的生活水平，为人们提供了改善生活的机会。医学和技术的突破对个人和社区都产生了巨大的影响。任何人类里程碑式的进步都得益于技术突破。技术创新通常与全球经济增长和发展密切相关。
questions.answer: Technological innovation is a fundamental driver of economic growth and human progress. It has taken center stage in a technological age and plays a central role in the global economy. Advances in technology contribute to higher levels of economic output and can deliver new goods and services that change human lives and capabilities. New and innovative products have raised the standard of living and provided people with opportunities to improve their lives. Breakthroughs in medicine and technology have had a dramatic impact on individuals and communities alike. Any milestone in human progress benefits from technological breakthroughs. Technological innovation is generally closely associated with global growth and development.

questions.content: Write an essay on how to best handle the relationship between parents and children. You should write at least 120 words but no more than 180 words.
It is widely accepted that a balanced diet is _______ to health.
He showed courage and determination in the face of _______.
He will get over it — young people have amazing _______.
Long prison sentences can be a very _______ deterrent for offenders.
Many foods are suspected of being _______ to health because of the chemicals and additives they contain.
This valve _______ the flow of water into the river.
There is a(n) _______ debate on the issue of worldwide climate change.
We should try to see the issue from a different _______.
Most experts _______ caution in such health-related cases.
She did not feel under any _______ to tell him the truth.
He wasn't able to _______ the stresses and strains of the job.
If you go on like this you'll _______ in prison.
_______, the two systems are identical.
She was _______ severe money problems.
Her speech made a profound _______ everyone.
The recession is _______ the housing markets.
This tool can be used in _______ ways.
It was getting late when she finally _______.
We find that people imitate not only the particulars of positive actions, but also the spirit _______ them.
Not everyone can afford to donate to _______ or spend weeks on a service trip to Haiti.
Our work suggests that an individual's kindness can nonetheless _______ people to spread positivity in other ways.
In our study, people were given a $1 "bonus" in _______ to their payment for completing the study.
After making each donation, participants saw what they believed was the average _______ made by the last 100 people in the study.
Some people learned that they lived in a _______ world, where people donated about three quarters of their bonus to charity.
Like other scientists, we found that _______ who believed others were generous became more generous themselves.
We then tested our real question: does kindness contagion transcend mere _______?
People who had watched others donate generously wrote friendlier, more _______ and more supportive notes than those who had watched others behave greedily.
This suggests that kindness _______ as it diffuses, "infecting" the behaviors through which new individuals can express it.
Studies show that having a strong sense of purpose protects us from stress in the short term and predicts long-term better health, a lower risk of dying prematurely and even better financial health.
questions.answer: 研究表明，有强烈的使命感不仅在短期内保护我们免受压力的困扰，而且会让我们获得长远的健康，降低过早死亡的风险，并带来更好的财政状况。

questions.content: Feeling responsible for other people also can help us cope with whatever challenges life brings.
questions.answer: 对他人有责任感也可以帮助我们应对生活中的各种挑战。

questions.content: She noted that caring for others helps us regulate our own emotions and gain a sense of control.
questions.answer: 她指出关心他人可以帮助我们调节自己的情绪并获得掌控感。

questions.content: 友善，顾名思义，就是在人际交往中表现得友好而善良。从广义上来讲，友善包含善待亲友、他人和自然。善待亲友可以和谐家庭关系；善待他人可以和谐人际关系；善待自然可以形成和谐的生态关系。能否以友善的态度为人处事，不仅体现着一个人的道德水平，还体现了社会的和谐程度。作为中华民族千百年形成的基本传统美德，友善在社会生活中发挥着不可替代的作用。
questions.answer: Kindness, just as its name implies, is being friendly and kind in interpersonal relationships. In a broad sense, kindness refers to being kind to family, friends, strangers and nature. Being kind to family and friends can strengthen family ties; being kind to strangers can bring people closer to each other; being kind to nature can create harmonious ecological relationships. Whether we behave in a friendly manner not only reflects an individual's moral standards, but also reflects the degree of social harmony. As a basic traditional virtue that was formed by the Chinese nation over thousands of years, kindness plays an irreplaceable role in its society.

questions.content: Write an essay on Should the Library be Open to the Public in the University? You should write at least 120 words but no more than 180 words.
